Text contains formal 'Demande à formuler' with explicit deadlines ('Dans un délai de un mois', 'Dans un délai de 5 mois') for document transmission -- matches S02_INJONCTION, not S01_CONSTAT.

Record explicitly references 'FSMD 4 de la précédente inspection du 19/11/2020' (prior threshold-exceedance finding on the same déchetterie) -- T1 (no history mentioned) is factually wrong; this is an explicit follow-up (T2_SUIVI).

Hunter's own reasoning states 'MED active + astreinte en prescription' concurrent with an on-site NC technique (batardeaux/étanchéité) -- matches the G4 heuristic (MED active + NC technique concomitante), not G3.
M07_CONTROLE is not supported by constats_body -- no vérification/contrôle périodique is described, only a physical NC (M14). Spurious multi-label.
Text states 'Si la requalification n'est pas conforme les équipements seront immédiatement mis à l'arrêt' -- a conditional threat (m_MENACE under-application pattern), missed by Hunter.

error_patterns: 1) Code-validity contamination (53/130 records, ~41%): Hunter duplicated m_DELAI/m_MENACE modifiers inside the `mechanisms` array in addition to `modifiers`, violating the STRICT rule that modifiers go only in `modifiers`. Purely mechanical, fixed by stripping m_* entries from `mechanisms` everywhere. 2) m_DELAI over-application (15 records): Hunter attached m_DELAI whenever a 'Demande à formuler' section existed, even when the demand carried no numeric/dated deadline (e.g. 'transmet dès réception', 'doit fournir', with no 'sous X mois/jours'). Concentrated in the siaepa-cubzadais and sibelco/siap-sarp sub-batches. 3) m_MENACE under-application (1 record): a conditional-threat clause phrased as an equipment shutdown risk ('seront immédiatement mis à l'arrêt') rather than the standard 'suites administratives et pénales' boilerplate was missed; all standard-phrasing menace clauses were correctly tagged. 4) One S01/S02 stage miss where an explicit dual deadline ('délai de un mois' / 'délai de 5 mois') was present in the demand but Hunter left stage at S01_CONSTAT. 5) One T1/T2 trajectory miss where the record explicitly cites a prior inspection's finding (FSMD4 2020) but was left at the T1 default. 6) One gravity/mechanism miss where Hunter's own reasoning text asserted 'MED active' concurrent with a physical NC but still assigned G3 instead of G4, and paired it with a spurious M07 tag not supported by constats_body. No instances found of: R10_TENSION misuse, R02 over-attribution on factual statements, false M08, false M14, over-aggressive M17, or missed multi-label mechanisms -- these Hunter patterns, common in other batches, were not present here.
verdict: Hunter's substantive classification judgment (domain, mechanism selection beyond the m_* contamination, dynamic, gravity, trajectory) was strong: 73/130 records (56%) required no correction at all, and of the 57 records touched, 53 needed only the mechanical m_* mechanisms/modifiers cleanup with no other field affected. 0 M15_AUTRE. The dominant issue is structural/mechanical (m_* codes duplicated into `mechanisms`) rather than a judgment error, and the second most common issue (m_DELAI applied without textual deadline support, 15 records) is a narrow, easily-targeted overreach concentrated in a few sub-batches. Only 4 records had a genuine substantive misclassification (gravity, stage, trajectory, or missed m_MENACE). Batch is fit for merge after these corrections.
